questions.text: If x and y are in direct proportion, then:
questions.explanation: In direct proportion, x/y = k (constant). As x increases, y increases proportionally.

questions.text: In inverse proportion, product of two variables is constant.
questions.explanation: In inverse proportion, xy = k (constant). As x increases, y decreases.

questions.text: Give an example of direct proportion.
questions.correctAnswer: More articles, more cost. Speed and distance (for fixed time).
questions.explanation: Direct proportion examples: cost increases with quantity, distance increases with speed (for same time).

questions.text: If 5 workers complete work in 10 days, 10 workers will complete in ________ days.
questions.explanation: This is inverse proportion. More workers = less time. 5 × 10 = 10 × x, so x = 5 days.
